title: More People Take on Multiple Jobs as Living Costs Rise
subtitle: From young workers to retirees, many are finding that one job is no longer enough to meet their needs or desires.

summary.en: Across the UK, a growing number of people are taking on multiple jobs to manage rising living costs and achieve financial stability. This trend affects young professionals struggling to enter their chosen fields, as well as older workers who find their state pensions are not enough to cover basic expenses. The rise of the 'gig economy' and changes in industries, including the impact of artificial intelligence, are also pushing people towards more flexible but often less secure work. While some older individuals choose to work longer for social connection and mental stimulation, many others, especially women, are forced to continue working due to financial pressures. Experts note that this multi-job workforce highlights wider issues of affordability, job security, and the changing nature of employment.
summary.zh: 在英國各地，越來越多的人身兼多職，以應對不斷上漲的生活成本並實現財務穩定。這種趨勢影響著努力進入所選領域的年輕專業人士，以及發現國家養老金不足以支付基本開支的年長勞工。「零工經濟」的興起以及產業變革，包括人工智慧的影響，也促使人們轉向更彈性但通常較不穩定的工作。雖然一些年長者選擇工作更長時間是為了社交連結和心智刺激，但許多其他人，特別是女性，則因經濟壓力而被迫繼續工作。專家指出，這種身兼多職的勞動力凸顯了負擔能力、工作保障和就業性質變化等更廣泛的問題。

paragraphs.en: More than a million people in the UK are now working more than one job, a clear sign of how rising costs, job insecurity, and changes in different industries are affecting workers. Many are turning to the 'gig economy' – a system where people do short-term, freelance, or contract work instead of having a permanent job – to make ends meet or build a more secure future.
paragraphs.zh: 英國目前有超過一百萬人身兼多職，這清楚顯示了不斷上漲的生活成本、工作不穩定性以及各行各業的變革，如何影響著勞工。許多人轉向「零工經濟」（一種人們從事短期、自由接案或合約工作，而非擁有固定職位的體系），以維持生計或建立更穩定的未來。

paragraphs.en: Billy-Jo Pierce, 29, from Bristol, is one example. She told BBC Business that she feels like she is "living in survival mode." Pierce works 50-60 hours a week, running her own business decorating teeth with cosmetic gems, while also taking shifts at receptions, working in bars, at festivals, and selling clothes online. She loves her work but admits that the "burn out is real." After finishing university with a good degree, she struggled to find work in her field of interior design. She took a full-time office job and built her business in the evenings, often working until 11 pm. Pierce felt she was "part of a massive rat race" and was working hard but not saving money or truly living. After losing her office job last year, she decided to focus on her business full-time. However, the high cost of living in Bristol and increasing material costs in the beauty industry, which BBC Business reports have risen by over 90% in ten years, made it hard to live on one income. To save money, Pierce now lives in a van and still takes on several jobs to support her business. She constantly worries about money and feels that working multiple jobs is now "quite the norm."
paragraphs.zh: 29歲的布里斯托居民比利-喬·皮爾斯 (Billy-Jo Pierce) 就是其中一例。她告訴 BBC 商業新聞，她感覺自己「活在求生模式中」。皮爾斯每週工作 50 到 60 小時，經營自己的牙齒美容飾品事業，同時也在接待處輪班、在酒吧和節慶活動工作，並在網路上販售服飾。她熱愛自己的工作，但也承認「職業倦怠是真實存在的」。大學畢業並取得好學位後，她卻難以在室內設計領域找到工作。她找了一份全職辦公室工作，並在晚上發展自己的事業，經常工作到晚上 11 點。皮爾斯覺得自己「身處一場巨大的老鼠賽跑中」，努力工作卻存不到錢，也無法真正享受生活。去年失去辦公室工作後，她決定全職投入自己的事業。然而，布里斯托高昂的生活成本以及美容產業不斷上漲的材料成本（根據 BBC 商業新聞報導，十年內已上漲超過 90%），讓她難以僅靠一份收入生活。為了省錢，皮爾斯現在住在廂型車裡，並且仍然身兼數職來支持她的事業。她不斷為錢煩惱，覺得身兼多職現在「相當普遍」。

paragraphs.en: The Office for National Statistics data, cited by BBC Business, shows that around 1.3 million people in the UK currently have a second job. This is a small drop from a record high of 1.35 million in 2025. The UK unemployment rate has recently gone up to 5%, and the number of available jobs has fallen to its lowest point in five years. At the same time, the gig economy has grown significantly, with almost five million people now doing gig work, such as food delivery, freelance design, or cleaning. However, only about one-fifth of these people rely on gig work as their main income. When unemployment increases, more people often look for gig work. For many, especially younger workers, having just one employer no longer feels safe.
paragraphs.zh: 根據 BBC 商業新聞引述的國家統計局資料顯示，英國目前約有 130 萬人有第二份工作。這比 2025 年創紀錄的 135 萬人略有下降。英國失業率最近已上升至 5%，而職位空缺數量已降至五年來的最低點。同時，零工經濟顯著成長，目前約有五百萬人從事零工工作，例如外送、自由接案設計或清潔。然而，其中只有約五分之一的人將零工工作作為主要收入來源。當失業率上升時，更多人通常會尋找零工工作。對許多人來說，特別是年輕勞工，只擁有一位雇主不再感到安全。

paragraphs.en: Industry changes are also a factor. Engy Elboreini, a freelance graphic designer from Bristol, told BBC Business that she has had to change her work because of new technology. She explained that tools like artificial intelligence (AI) and easy-to-use design programs mean her clients can now do much of the design work themselves. This has made the last two years her most difficult for business.
paragraphs.zh: 產業變革也是一個因素。來自布里斯托的自由平面設計師恩吉·艾爾伯雷尼 (Engy Elboreini) 告訴 BBC 商業新聞，由於新科技的出現，她不得不改變工作方式。她解釋說，人工智慧 (AI) 等工具和易於使用的設計軟體，意味著她的客戶現在可以自行完成大部分設計工作。這使得過去兩年成為她事業上最艱難的時期。

paragraphs.en: This trend is not only affecting younger workers. Many older people are also working longer. Mandy Kemp, 70, from Dover, still works three days a week as a practice manager for a financial adviser. She told BBC Business that her state pension alone would not cover her rent and living costs, especially as she supports her husband who cannot work due to health issues. Kemp enjoys her job but feels "very uncertain" about the future, saying she doesn't see how she would survive without working.
paragraphs.zh: 這種趨勢不僅影響年輕勞工。許多老年人也工作更長時間。70 歲的多佛居民曼蒂·坎普 (Mandy Kemp) 仍然每週工作三天，擔任財務顧問的業務經理。她告訴 BBC 商業新聞，她的國家養老金不足以支付房租和生活費，特別是她還要扶養因健康問題無法工作的丈夫。坎普喜歡她的工作，但對未來感到「非常不確定」，她說她不知道如果沒有工作該如何生存。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Andrea Barry from the Centre for Ageing Better, a not-for-profit group, noted that "more and more" people have been working longer over the past two decades. She explained that people are living longer, and the official age for receiving a state pension has increased. Dr. Barry added that women are often more affected because they are more likely to have had part-time jobs without pensions or have had caring duties that stopped them from working. While many older people want to work past the traditional retirement age, some have "no choice" but to continue working due to the cost of living, a lack of savings, and rising rents. Dr. Barry also pointed out that older workers contribute to the economy, especially if they have skills that employers need. Working can also be good for health and wellbeing, helping older workers stay active and avoid loneliness, though she admitted it could be harmful for others, describing some in their 60s and 70s as "very precarious and vulnerable."
paragraphs.zh: 非營利組織「改善高齡生活中心」的安德莉亞·巴里博士 (Dr. Andrea Barry) 指出，過去二十年來，「越來越多」的人工作時間更長。她解釋說，人們壽命更長，而且領取國家養老金的法定年齡也提高了。巴里博士補充說，女性通常受影響更大，因為她們更有可能從事沒有養老金的兼職工作，或者因為照顧責任而無法工作。雖然許多老年人希望在傳統退休年齡之後繼續工作，但由於生活成本、缺乏儲蓄和租金上漲，有些人「別無選擇」只能繼續工作。巴里博士也指出，老年勞工對經濟有所貢獻，特別是如果他們擁有雇主所需的技能。工作也有益於健康和福祉，幫助老年勞工保持活躍並避免孤獨，儘管她承認這對其他人可能有害，她形容一些六、七十歲的人「非常不穩定且脆弱」。

paragraphs.en: However, not all older workers continue for financial reasons. Jackie Haynes, who will be 80 this year, loves her part-time job as an activity coordinator at a care home. She told BBC Business that she doesn't need the money because she has private pensions and owns her home. For her, working gives her purpose, helps her feel socially connected, and "keeps me young." Similarly, Mike Sandford, 78, from Redhill, is worried about the mental impact if he retires from his full-time job as a mechanical design engineer. He needs something to "stimulate the mind" and be creative, feeling that his hobbies alone would not be enough to keep him active and engaged.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，並非所有老年勞工都是出於經濟原因而繼續工作。今年將滿 80 歲的潔姬·海恩斯 (Jackie Haynes) 熱愛她在安養院擔任活動協調員的兼職工作。她告訴 BBC 商業新聞，她不需要這筆錢，因為她有私人養老金並擁有自己的房子。對她來說，工作給予她目標，幫助她感受到社會連結，並且「讓她保持年輕」。同樣地，78 歲的雷德希爾居民麥克·桑福德 (Mike Sandford) 擔心如果他從全職機械設計工程師的工作退休，會對心理產生影響。他需要一些東西來「刺激思維」並保持創造力，他覺得單靠嗜好不足以讓他保持活躍和投入。

paragraphs.en: Overall, the rise of the multi-job workforce shows a complex picture in the UK. While some choose to work longer for personal fulfillment, a significant number, both young and old, are taking on extra jobs out of necessity, highlighting ongoing challenges with affordability, job security, and the changing nature of work.
paragraphs.zh: 總體而言，身兼多職勞動力的興起呈現出英國複雜的局面。雖然有些人選擇工作更長時間是為了個人成就感，但有相當多的人，無論年輕或年長，都是出於必要而身兼多職，這凸顯了在負擔能力、工作保障和工作性質變化方面持續存在的挑戰。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To earn just enough money to cover one's basic expenses.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 勉強維持生計；收支平衡。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語表示收入僅夠支付基本開銷。文中：Many are turning to the 'gig economy' – a system where people do short-term, freelance, or contract work instead of having a permanent job – to make ends meet or build a more secure future.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become extremely tired or sick from working too hard over a period of time.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （因工作過度而）身心俱疲；耗盡精力。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語表示因過度勞累而感到筋疲力盡。文中：She loves her work but admits that the "burn out is real."

keyTerms.definitionEn: A way of life in which people are caught up in a fiercely competitive struggle for wealth or power.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （為財富或權力而進行的）激烈競爭；你爭我奪的局面。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語比喻人們在社會中為追求財富或成功而進行的無休止的競爭。文中：Pierce felt she was "part of a massive rat race" and was working hard but not saving money or truly living.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Not safe or certain; likely to fall or become dangerous.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 不穩定的；不確定的；危險的。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞在此處形容生活或經濟狀況不穩定，容易陷入困境。文中：Working can also be good for health and wellbeing, helping older workers stay active and avoid loneliness, though she admitted it could be harmful for others, describing some in their 60s and 70s as "very precarious and vulnerable."

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: 'I live in survival mode': The rise of the multi-job workforce

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: 'I don't think I'll ever be able to retire'

quiz.question: According to the article, what specific percentage increase has there been in material costs for the beauty industry over the past ten years, as reported by BBC Business?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案 B，因為文章第二段明確提到：「...the high cost of living in Bristol and increasing material costs in the beauty industry, which BBC Business reports have risen by over 90% in ten years, made it hard to live on one income.」這直接指出美容行業的材料成本在十年內上漲了超過 90%。選項 A、C 和 D 均與文章中提供的具體數據不符。

quiz.question: Based on the information about Engy Elboreini and Dr. Andrea Barry, what can be inferred about the future job market for certain professions?

options.A: Traditional office jobs will become more secure due to increased demand.
options.B: The demand for highly specialized manual labor will significantly decrease.
options.C: Professions susceptible to automation or readily available digital tools may face increasing challenges.
options.D: Older workers will be entirely replaced by younger, technologically savvy individuals.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案 C。文章第四段提到 Engy Elboreini 作為自由平面設計師，因 AI 和易用設計工具的出現，客戶能自行完成設計工作，導致她業務困難。這暗示了容易被自動化或數位工具取代的職業將面臨挑戰。選項 A 不對，文章並未提及傳統辦公室工作會更安全。選項 B 不對，文章未討論手工勞動的未來。選項 D 是一種過度推斷，文章提及年長者工作時間變長，但沒有說他們會被年輕人完全取代，而是強調了技能和經濟需求。

quiz.question: In the context of Dr. Andrea Barry's statement, what does the word 'precarious' most closely mean when describing some older workers in their 60s and 70s?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案 A。在文章第六段中，Dr. Andrea Barry 描述一些 60 多歲和 70 多歲的年長者為「very precarious and vulnerable」。在這裡，'precarious' 指的是他們的生活或經濟狀況不穩定、不確定，可能隨時面臨困難。這與上下文提及的「生活成本、缺乏儲蓄和租金上漲」等因素相符。選項 B (謹慎細緻) 不符合語境。選項 C (要求高且困難) 描述的是工作性質，而非個人處境。選項 D (有價值且有經驗) 與 'vulnerable' 的搭配意義相反。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the main argument of the article regarding the rise of the multi-job workforce in the UK?

options.A: The gig economy is the primary solution for all workers facing job insecurity and high living costs.
options.B: Most people are choosing to work multiple jobs purely for personal fulfillment and to stay active in retirement.
options.C: The UK's multi-job workforce is growing mainly because younger generations are unwilling to commit to single employers.
options.D: A significant number of UK workers, both young and old, are taking on multiple jobs out of economic necessity, though some do so for personal reasons, reflecting complex challenges in the job market.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案 D。文章的結論段明確指出：「While some choose to work longer for personal fulfillment, a significant number, both young and old, are taking on extra jobs out of necessity, highlighting ongoing challenges with affordability, job security, and the changing nature of work.」這句話精確地概括了文章的核心論點：許多人是出於經濟必要性而兼職，但也有少數人是為了個人成就。選項 A 過於簡化，文章指出兼職是應對挑戰的方式之一，但並非唯一的「解決方案」。選項 B 忽略了文章中大量關於經濟壓力的案例。選項 C 將原因歸結為年輕人的不願承諾，這只是文章提及的一個小部分因素，並非主要原因，且忽略了年長工作者的情況。
